ksa_text: Ability to design data systems, databases, and integration mechanisms, determining data requirements and managing data flows.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of infrastructure supporting information technology (IT) for safety, performance, and reliability.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of infrastructure supporting information technology (IT) for safety, performance, and reliability.
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Technical Foundations KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.441) to justify inclusion for the VP Data Strategy and Operations. Without stronger framework alignment, this ability KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain ability KSA be expected in real-world VP Data Strategy and Operations roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Technical Foundations is not a standard competency expectation for VP Data Strategy and Operations. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the VP Data Strategy and Operations competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.441) and edge-domain placement (Technical Foundations) do not meet inclusion threshold for the VP Data Strategy and Operations. All passes support exclusion.
